summary: A lightweight, stateless microservice that provides RESTful endpoints for validating email addresses and phone numbers using regex-based rules. Designed for high throughput, accuracy, and maintainability with no external validation dependencies.

components.name: Validation Service
components.responsibility: Orchestrates email and phone number validation logic; handles HTTP requests, input sanitization, and response formatting.

components.name: Email Validator
components.responsibility: Implements RFC 5322-inspired regex validation for email format and domain syntax (no DNS lookup); trims whitespace and rejects malformed domains.

components.name: Phone Validator
components.responsibility: Validates international and local phone numbers using E.164-aligned regex patterns; normalizes whitespace and supports '+' prefix, country codes, and digit-only formats.

components.name: Health Checker
components.responsibility: Exposes liveness and readiness endpoints for observability and orchestration.

api_endpoints.description: Validates a single email address against format and domain syntax rules.
Validates a single phone number against E.164-aligned format rules.
Validates up to 100 email and/or phone entries in a single request.

tech_stack.cache: None (optional Redis layer can be added later for rate-limiting or result caching)

deployment_strategy: Containerized deployment via Docker on Kubernetes with horizontal pod autoscaling (HPA) based on CPU and request rate; zero-downtime rolling updates; health probes configured for liveness/readiness.
security_considerations: Input sanitization (whitespace trimming, length limiting to 256 chars per field)
Rate limiting per IP (via middleware) to prevent abuse
No logging of raw PII in production (masked logs only)
No authentication required for public API (assumed internal or trusted consumer)
scalability_notes: Stateless design enables linear horizontal scaling; each instance handles ~5K RPS on modest hardware; batch endpoint reduces network overhead for bulk validation; future sharding possible via consistent hashing if distributed validation cache is introduced.
